perturbation.original_text: V. OBLIGATIONS . The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.changed_text: V. OBLIGATIONS . The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Both Parties shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that neither party can publish, copy, or use confidential information for their sole benefit without written approval of the other party. The modified text changes this to say that both parties shall publish, copy or use the Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OBLIGATIONS . The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Both Parties shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party could publish, copy, or use confidential information for their sole benefit without written approval. The changed text alters this to state that both parties shall publish, copy or use the Confidential Information for their sole benefit, directly contradicting the original restriction. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, reflects this contradiction. Therefore, the changed text introduces a clear and direct conflict regarding the permitted use of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.changed_text: This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Mutual as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Section V does not apply to the 1st Party if the agreement is Unilateral. The changed text says Section V doesn't apply if the agreement is Mutual. This contradicts the initial agreement, and creates in-text contradiction, which is creating a loophole for information breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OBLIGATIONS . The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Both Parties shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within 5 days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Mutual as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Section V does not apply to the 1st Party if the agreement is Unilateral. The changed text alters this to say that Section V doesn't apply if the agreement is Mutual. The contradicted text includes the statement: 'This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Mutual as marked in Sect ion II.' Because the original text stated that Section V does not apply if the agreement is Unilateral, and the changed text changes it to Mutual, it introduces a contradiction. If the agreement is in fact Unilateral, the clause in the Contradicted Text says that it does not apply to the 1st party if the agreement is Mutual. This creates an in-text contradiction, as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and is an example of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: VI. TIME PERIOD . The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.changed_text: VI. TIME PERIOD . The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect for 1 year, irrespective of whether such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the time period for maintaining confidentiality as lasting until the information is no longer a trade secret or written notice is provided. This perturbation changes the time period to a fixed term of 1 year. This contradicts the initial condition where the party is bounded from keeping confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TIME PERIOD . The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect for 1 year, irrespective of whether such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for the confidentiality period to end when the information is no longer a trade secret or written notice is given. The changed text fixes the confidentiality period to 1 year, irrespective of whether the information remains a trade secret or notice is given. The contradicted text is a copy of the Changed Text, which ensures there is a contradiction as it breaks the original unbounded clause. Therefore, a contradiction exists because the changed text makes the confidentiality period a fixed term, conflicting with the initial unbounded period.

perturbation.original_text: IX. ENFORCEMENT . The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.changed_text: IX. ENFORCEMENT . The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to remedies available at law, except punitive damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows the harmed party to seek all remedies available at law. The changed clause restricts this by excluding punitive damages. This contradicts the original statement and could affect enforcement depending on the applicable law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENFORCEMENT . The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to remedies available at law, except punitive damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the harmed party is entitled to "all remedies available at law". The changed text then limits this by excluding punitive damages. This creates a direct conflict within the contract, as one section allows for all remedies while another restricts it. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text in this case, highlighting that the changed text itself introduces the contradiction by modifying what was previously allowable. The change directly alters the scope of available remedies, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
